message:
We keep resisting: US and Canada sanction Samidoun

Glory to the martyr, the struggler, Mahmoud Abu al-Rub, "Abu al-Fashk" (photo 1), one of the leaders & fighters of the Al-Quds Brigades - Qabatiya. Like so many of the great martyrs of Palestine, he was a liberated prisoner, released 5 months ago from 4 years in the occupation's jails (images 3+4).

He was martyred during a heroic armed confrontation with the invading occupation forces that attacked the Al-Sibat neighborhood in Jenin city and murdered 17-year-old Rayan al-Sayed (image 2). 

Glory and immortality to the martyr; victory to the resistance; liberation for Palestine.

Occupation forces have abducted Jamal Banat from al-Khalil, the cousin of the martyr Nizar Banat, known for his relentless commitment to the resistance and his rejection of the Oslo "Palestinian" Authority, today from Jenin refugee camp after he was injured.

Jamal Banat was being pursued by both the PA under its "security coordination" regime & the occupation forces themselves. He repeatedly stated his commitment to Nizar Banat's path, and is now imprisoned for that commitment.

Free Jamal Banat & all Palestinian prisoners!

Translated from the original French of @samidounparisbanlieue

The memory of the martyrs accompanies the living through the long road to Liberation, and their portraits, lovingly painted on the walls of cities, villages and refugee camps throughout historic Palestine, watch over the new generation of resisters, over the children whose laughter bounces off the stones of houses, over the elders who watch with their loving eyes, their people proudly and courageously resisting colonization. Generation after generation, until liberation. 

The beloved martyrs, assassinated in bombings, from Gaza to Beirut, burned in tents and whose screams tear the night, whose blood mixes with sacks of flour. Whose bodies are crushed by bombs or objects dropped from the sky. 

The beloved martyrs, who fell with weapons in their hands, defending their people in Gaza, the West Bank and throughout historic Palestine. The beloved martyrs, barefoot martyrs, with skin flayed by thorns and stones, who heroically confront the genocidal Zionist army, in the tunnels and rubble of Gaza. The memory of the martyrs watches over the living throughout the long road to Liberation...

Imprisoned martyrs, whose bodies are frozen in the morgues and refrigerators of the occupation, and who wait to finally join the land trodden by the feet of their beloved people. Beloved martyrs, imprisoned martyrs, frozen martyrs.

The memory of the martyrs accompanies the living throughout the long road to liberation, proud martyrs, whose streets remember laughter, stories and feats of arms. Homage to the martyrs, glory to the Palestinian and Lebanese resistance, Palestine Will Win.

On Saturday, 12 October, @samidounparisbanlieue took to the streets in Paris’ 18th arrondissement, together with Urgence Palestine 18,  MIRA, and 18eme en lutte, calling for the liberation of Khalida Jarrar and all Palestinian prisoners, support for the Palestinian resistance, resisting French fascism, and a free Palestine from the river to the sea.

Palestinian scholar, feminist, leftist and former parliamentarian Khalida Jarrar is currently being held in solitary confinement in the Zionist Neve Tirza prison in extremely harsh conditions. She has been held in isolation for over two months now. Like her detention itself — she is jailed without charge or trial under administrative detention, alongside nearly 4,000 of the 10,100 Palestinians imprisoned by the occupation regime — no reason or excuse has been proffered by the occupation authorities to justify her ongoing isolation.

She is held in a very small solitary confinement cell, measuring only 2 meters by 1.5 meters, where the only available space is occupied by a mattress. The cell also contains a space with a toilet and a shower and is completely closed, with no windows for ventilation or fresh air. 

In a message from Neve Tirza, conveyed by her lawyers, Jarrar said:

“I die every day. The cell is like a small closed box where no air enters. There is only a toilet in the cell with a small window above it, which was later sealed just one day after my transfer. They left me no space to breathe, and even what is called the ‘ashnav’ (peephole) on the cell door was sealed. There is only a small opening where I sit most of the time to breathe. I am suffocating in my cell, waiting for the hours to pass, hoping to find some oxygen particles to breathe and stay alive.”

Jarrar, who suffers from several health problems, continued:

“What worsened the misery of my isolation is the high temperatures. I am, in short, inside an oven at its highest setting. I cannot sleep due to the extreme heat, and not only have they isolated me in these conditions, but they have deliberately cut off the water in the cell. Even when I request to fill a water bottle to drink, they bring it after at least four hours. As for going out to the prison yard, I was allowed only once after eight days of isolation, and they deliberately delay the delivery of the poor-quality meal for hours.”

The march concluded with a general meeting with speeches and activities; participants also wrote letters to Palestinian prisoners, including Khalida Jarrar and other Palestinian women prisoners as part of the @dismantle_damon campaign. Samidoun Paris Banlieue and the organizers urged all to participate massively in the annual international march to Lannemezan prison on 26 October, for the liberation of Georges Abdallah on the 40th anniversary of his arrest in France. The 72-year-old Lebanese Arab communist and committed struggler for Palestine will mark 40 years in French prison this month. Join the buses and caravans to Lannemezan from Toulouse, Paris, Bordeaux, Marseille, Grenoble and many other cities to free Georges Abdallah!

The illegitimate occupation “military court” date for Al-Quds University medical student Ahmed Thabet Abu Khdeir has been postponed until 5 January 2025. Ahmed was abducted by Zionist occupation forces on 24 April 2023 during a violent raid on his family house  in his hometown of Surif, northwest of Al-Khalil. His arrest was carried out amidst a number of settler attacks against his  community, supported and protected by the IOF.

Ahmed was just two weeks away from submitting his graduation project in physical therapy when he was abducted by the occupation. The raid on his home lasted for 4 hours, as the invading soldiers smashed his family’s property, destroyed their garden, and beat and cursed Ahmed and his family. 

Ahmed memorized the entire Qur’an during the 18 months of his incarceration. His “military court” trial has been repeatedly postponed, often used by the occupation as another form of harassment against Palestinian prisoners.

Free Ahmed Abu Khdeir and all Palestinian students!

📌 US Involvement in the Attempted Assassination of Hezbollah Chief Wafiq Safa
 
In today’s article, Ibrahim Al-Amin delves into alarming claims by senior Lebanese security officials, suggesting possible US involvement in an Israeli airstrike targeting Hezbollah's Wafiq Safa. Safa, head of Hezbollah’s Liaison and Coordination Unit, was the alleged target of the airstrike in Beirut last Thursday. Although Hezbollah denied that Safa was hit, suspicions about Washington’s role in the assassination attempt have intensified.
 
According to three senior Lebanese officials, Western intelligence services, led by the US, have maintained direct contact with Lebanon’s military and security institutions since the outbreak of open war between Lebanon and “Israel”. The officials revealed that US intelligence has been especially focused on Hezbollah’s communications with Lebanese authorities during the conflict. One official noted that Hezbollah had instructed Safa to maintain contact with Lebanese military and security officials despite the risks involved. This communication reportedly became the focal point for US and Israeli intelligence, culminating in the airstrike on Beirut.
 
The airstrike has heightened Hezbollah’s belief that the US played a role in orchestrating the attempt on Safa's life. Although Safa holds no military role, making him an unlikely target, Israeli forces reportedly carried out the strike, potentially on behalf of Washington. One Lebanese security official stated, “Israel has no objection to assassinating Safa; after all, he was once an assistant to Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah. But this operation seems driven by US intelligence, aimed at severing Hezbollah’s ties to Lebanon’s military and security officials”.
 
The official went on to explain that the US might be signaling its intent to undermine Hezbollah’s influence by targeting non-military figures, like Safa, whose connections could apply pressure on Lebanese authorities. Furthermore, the assassination attempt is seen as part of a broader campaign, spearheaded by US Ambassador to Beirut Lisa Johnson, who has reportedly urged Lebanese political forces to prepare for a “post-Hezbollah era”.
 
Compounding these suspicions, on October 10, a team of 15 CIA officers arrived in Beirut and was immediately transported to the US embassy in Awkar. This new cell joined the existing CIA operation in Lebanon, which includes 12 key officers tasked with overseeing intelligence gathering and coordinating actions. These operations, cloaked under the guise of counterterrorism, aim to disrupt Hezbollah’s communications and influence.
 
One Lebanese security official revealed that US intelligence’s influence in Lebanon has evolved dramatically over the past decade, particularly with the appointment of Sherry Baker as the CIA’s new Beirut station chief. Baker has participated in several meetings with Lebanese security officials during official visits to Washington, and five Lebanese officers have made work trips to the CIA’s Langley headquarters in recent months.
 
This investigation raises significant questions about the extent of US involvement in the targeting of Hezbollah figures and the broader implications of such actions. By potentially supporting Israeli operations against Hezbollah’s non-military personnel, the US may be signaling a shift in its strategy, aiming to destabilize the movement’s ties to Lebanese institutions while exerting pressure on Hezbollah’s political influence.

Full article by Ibrahim al-Amin, editor-in-chief of Al-Akhbar, can be found in Arabic here:
https://www.al-akhbar.com/Lebanon/385413

On the day of October 7, Intisar Hijazi, a 41 year old school teacher, from the town of Tamra in occupied Palestine ‘48, was kidnapped by the occupation forces for ”incitement to terrorism.” They blindfolded her and later, Itamar Ben Gvir posted on his social media a photo of her blindfolded in a police car, taking credit for her arrest. Another photo of Intisar was leaked on social media; this time, she was standing blindfolded in front of an ”israeli” flag.

On 7th of October 2023, at the first hours of the morning, Intisar made a video of her dancing and posted it on TikTok. This year, she reposted the video as an ”on this day” memory. The police acted according to Ben-Gvir’s direction, detaining her for 72 hours, after which she was released. Her treatment is emblematic of the systemic racism and repression directed against Palestinian citizens of the Zionist regime. 

Intisar described the horrible conditions she was put through during her arrest. In the beginning, she asked the police to not blindfold and handcuff her, as she will come with them willingly. The police officer ignored her and blindfolded and handcuffed her brutally. They also ripped her clothes off and searched her in a very brutal way. They did not let her use the bathroom and told her to ”do it on herself”.  But the one thing that they insisted on was for her to ”dance” even if she constantly told them that she is not a dancer and does not want to do it.

Nour Abu Zaid is a student at Birzeit University. On the 6th of October, she was kidnapped by the occupation forces near the town of Rawabi and arrested. Her car was also stolen by the occupation forces.

Nour was released six days later, on the 12th of October. 

The practice of humiliation and intimidation is the only tactic that the zionist entity knows how to use, because, when confronted with the steadfastness of the Palestinian prisoners, they have nothing else left.

On 13 October 2024, the illegitimate Zionist occupation “military court” extended the administrative detention -- imprisonment without charge or trial -- of Ahmed Saed al-Rimawi, an engineering student at Birzeit University, for another 4 months. Ahmed was abducted by occupation forces on 22 February 2024 from his home in Beit Rima, near Ramallah, and ordered to 4 months of administrative detention, which was renewed in June and again today. Free Ahmed al-Rimawi and all Palestinian  students!

Afnan Abu Hussein, 24 and nine months pregnant with her second child and a scheduled caesarean section later this month, is finally free on cash bail of 5,000 NIS ($1300 USD/1200 EUR). She was abducted from her home in Bani Naim, near al-Khalil, yesterday by occupation forces. Despite an order for her to be released on bail, she was instead moved to prison and then hospitalized due to her delicate health conditions, in harsh conditions.

An ambulance received her at the Kfar Saba checkpoint near Qalqilya, and will take her to the hospital in Qalqilya to give her fluids before transferring her to al-Khalil. Afnan is also the mother of 2-year-old Ubaida. 

Welcome home, Afnan, and freedom for all Palestinian prisoners!

Occupation courts extended the administrative detention of  imprisoned Birzeit University student Moatasem Zalloum by 4 months. Arrested on October 20, he was transferred to administrative detention on October 
26 for a period of 6 months, which was extended for an additional 6 months last April.

Moatasem had already been arrested in December 2020 and released in November 2021. On the day of his release, the procession that welcomed him was attacked by members of the security services of the collaborating Palestinian Authority. He was arrested a second time in 2022, before being released in March 2023 after 10 months of administrative detention.

Free Moatasem Zalloum and all Palestinian prisoners!

On October 3rd 2024, occupation authorities handed Khadija Khweis, 45, a mother of five children from al-Tur neighborhood of occupied East Jerusalem, a decision to forbid her communication with a number of Palestinians.

Khadija is one of the murabitat of Al-Aqsa Mosque, the women who worship and hold Islamic religious education for other women at the mosque, while working to defend and protect it from Zionist settlers who seek to invade, perform rituals, claim and even destroy the holy site. 

The murabitat are heavily presecuted by the occupation for their activism, with frequent arrests and bans on entering the holy site.

During Ramadan 2015, in response to one of these bans, Hanadi al-Halawani and Khadija Khweis started a habit of flipping pots of maqluba to serve at the entrance of Al-Aqsa as a form of guard and protest. This dish, called the “dish of victory,” became integral part of Palestinian demonstrations in Jerusalem.

Khadija herself was banned for the first time from entering Al-Aqsa compound in 2011. Since then, this has happened repeatedly, for days or months at a time, for a total of over 8 years.

She was also banned from traveling abroad and separated from her husband who was forced to leave their home and move to the West Bank since he does not have residency papers for East Jerusalem.

Freedom and justice for the murabitat — defend al-Aqsa from colonialism!

Three Birzeit University students & recent graduates. are currently being detained by the Palestinian Authority under its “security coordination” with the Zionist occupation: Amr Aziz Kayed, Abdullah Rabaya, and Qassam Dalani. 

On 12 October 2024, Amr Kayed’s detention was extended for an additional week.

Amr Kayed was abducted by the Palestinian Authority on 16 September 2024. Two days later, on 18 September, they followed and shot at Abdullah Rabaya, and then arrested him on 24 September.  They then seized Qassam Dalani -- previously imprisoned by the occupation and a recent university graduate involved with the student movement -- on 25 September.   

10 days after his detention, the PA court system ordered Amr Kayed freed -- but the security agencies refused the order and have continued to detain him and his fellow students. In response, the student movement at Birzeit University launched a sit-in to demand an end to the PA’s policy of arrest and persecution of student activists.

All of these arrests and detentions come within the framework of “security coordination” -- under the Oslo Accords, which created the PA, the Authority is responsible for providing “security” to and collaborating with  the Zionist occupation. This includes pursuing and arresting  resistance fighters, seizing weapons of the  resistance, and attacking the student movement. 

Free all Palestinian students in Zionist, PA and imperialist prisons!

Palestinian student at An-Najah University, Abdullah Bashir Obaid, from Qalqilya, was freed today after one year of illegitimate colonial imprisonment without charge or trial in Zionist prisons. 

Salutes of freedom to Abdullah, and liberation now for all Palestinian prisoners in zionist, PA and imperialist jails!

On the morning of 5 August 2024, student activist and representative for the Islamic Bloc at Birzeit University, Hamza Jihad Hamed, was detained as part of a series of arrests targeted at student organisers. Hamza was arrested in Al-Bireh, adding to a long list of raids targeting the Islamic Bloc. Today, his military court “trial” was postponed until 24 November, the fifth such delay since his arrest. 

The arrest of Hamza comes in the context of ongoing occupation and PA efforts to repress the work of the Islamic Bloc and the student movement broadly in West Bank universities. Dozens of student leaders have been forcibly disappeared over the past year in attacks on campuses and the homes of student activists across the entire West Bank. Arrests have been intensified since the Al-Aqsa Flood operation and as of today at least 14 Islamic Bloc leaders from Birzeit are held in Zionist jails.

Freedom for Hamza Hamed and all Palestinian prisoners!

Palestinian journalist Rasha Herzallah, arrested by Zionist occupation forces on 2 June, is awaiting trial in the occupation’s illegitimate “military courts,” allegedly for “incitement” -- that is, speaking out publicly or on social media about the occupation and the Palestinian cause. 

Herzallah, 39, from Nablus, has had her detention repeatedly extended by occupation forces, and today, her trial was postponed once again, until 15 December 2024. 

Her brother, the martyr Mohammed Herzallah "Abu Hamdi", was one of the leaders of the Lions’ Den. He was martyred on 23 November 2022 from the injuries he received on 24 July of that year, during the assassination of fellow Lions’ Den founders, the martyrs Mohammed al-Azizi and Aboud Suboh He was also a former Palestinian prisoner who spent one and a half years in Zionist prisons.

Rasha Herzallah is one of nearly 100 Palestinian journalists detained in occupation prisons and one of five women journalists held in Damon prison. 

Bushra al-Tawil, from Al-Bireh, is a Palestinian journalist specializing in prisoners’ issues, who is currently held under administrative detention in Zionist jails. This is her seventh imprisonment by the occupation, after she was abducted again on 7 April 2024. 

Rula Hassanein, from Bethlehem, is detained since 19 March. Mother of a 9-month-old daughter who is sick and still breastfeeding, she worked for several media including Al Jazeera, BNFSJ and Radio Nisaa (a Palestinian women's radio station). A decision in favor of her release had been announced several times, but was never applied.

Amal Shujaiya, a journalism student at Birzeit University, was arrested in early June during a raid by colonial forces.

Palestinian journalist Israa Lafi was arrested on 17 July, then ordered to administrative detention for a period of 6 months days later, on 26 July. In 2018 she had already been arrested and spent 10 months in colonial prisons, and has published several articles on the situation of Palestinian women prisoners.

Free Rasha Herzallah and end the war on Palestinian journalists!

Palestinian student Ibrahim Al-Saadi from Jenin refugee camp, was abducted by the occupation forces at the illegitimate Homesh military checkpoint, as he was on his way to take an exam at An-Najah University.

Free Ibrahim al-Saadi and all Palestinian students!

Illegitimate occupation military courts extended the administrative detention — imprisonment without charge or trial — of imprisoned Birzeit University student Moatasem Zalloum, for an additional four months.

He was seized on 20 October 2023 by occupation forces and then ordered, 6 days later, to administrative detention for six months, renewed again in April, and then renewed again today for four additional months.

There are over 3,500 Palestinian prisoners, out of over 10,000 held by the occupation, held in administrative detention without charge or trial. Administrative detention was first introduced to Palestine by the British colonial mandate and then adopted by the Zionist regime. Palestinians are detained on the basis of a so-called "secret file" that is not revealed to either the prisoner or their lawyer.

Moatasem was previously detained by the occupation between December 2020 and November 2021. On the day of his release, the procession welcoming him was attacked by members of the security services of the collaborationist Palestinian Authority engaged in "security coordination" with the Zionist occupation.

He was arrested once again in 2022, before being released in March 2023 after another 10 months of administrative detention. He has spent nearly three years in total inside Zionist jails, without charge or trial. 

Free Moatasem Zalloum and all Palestinian prisoners!

Afnan Abu Hussein, 24, was abducted from her home in Bani Naim, east of al-Khalil, by occupation forces today, who invaded the town and stormed her family home. Afnan is 9 months pregnant and the mother of a 2-year-old child. Free Afnan Abu Hussein and all Palestinian prisoners now!

Palestinian prisoners' associations have announced the martyrdom of Mohammed Munir Musa, 37, of Bethlehem, a Palestinian prisoner detained in occupation jails since March 2023. The occupation is continuing, as it does with hundreds of martyrs, to detain his body. He was martyred on Friday evening in Soroka Hospital, where he was apparently moved from Ramon prison; he was married and the father of three children. 

The circumstances of his martyrdom are unclear; however, he did not suffer from health problems prior to his detention, and his martyrdom is clearly the responsibility of the Zionist regime and its ongoing and systematic crimes against the Palestinian prisoners, as part and parcel of its genocide against the Palestinian people, including medical neglect and abuse, starvation, beating and torture. 

At least 41 Palestinian prisoners have been martyred in Zionist jails since the beginning of the battle of Al-Aqsa Flood, in addition to many martyrs among detainees abducted from Gaza whose names and identities are being concealed by the genocidal occupation, the largest number of martyred prisoners in a single year in the history of the Zionist colonization of Palestine.

"Wartime Cafe" EP13: Khaled Barakat "The liberation of Palestine, the role of diaspora."

Laith Marouf talks to Khaled Barakat, Palestinian Canadian revolutionary organiser. They discuss Khaled's history of organising from Jerusalem, to the USA, to Canada and beyond, the role of the Diaspora in the liberation of Palestine, and the repression they face in the "West" today. 

Camera/editing: Hadi Hotait
Filmed: 09/10/2024

Imprisoned Palestinian Birzeit University student Walid al Tarifi was released by Zionist occupation after 11 months of administrative detention, imprisonment without charge or trial. Greetings of liberation to Walid and freedom soon for all Palestinian prisoners!

Illegitimate occupation forces renewed the administrative detention -- imprisonment without charge or trial -- of Birzeit University student Obaida Qatusa for 4 months. He has now already been imprisoned for a full year.

Este fin de semana salimos a la calle junto a nuestras compañeras de Abya Yala para recordar que el 12 de octubre no solo no celebramos ninguna hispanidad, sino que honramos la resistencia anticolonial que llevan más de 500 siglos desarrollando.

¡Desde Palestina al Wallmapu, viva la resistencia índígena!

On Monday, 7 October, Samah Bilal Hijjawi, 25, a freed Palestinian prisoner who had been liberated in November 2023 in the exchange accomplished by the Palestinian resistance, was abducted by occupation forces that stormed her home as they invaded Qalqilya. 

Samah was held under administrative detention without charge or trial at the time of her liberation. 

Samah was previously arrested on 17 May 2023 after being summoned for an interrogation with the occupation intelligence and an administrative detention order was issued against her for a period of 4 months. Just days before, on 11 May 2023, she had been summoned by the Palestinian Authority intelligence, which engages in “security coordination” with the occupation, for an interrogation about her social media posts.

Multiple Palestinian women liberated in the exchange have been re-arrested, including Hanan Barghouthi, Walaa Tanja, Dania Hanatsheh, Aseel Samih Khader, and Yasmeen Shaaban (who was released hours later), while both Fadwa Hamadeh and Nofouth Hammad were summoned for interrogation. All of these re-arrests come in direct violation of the prisoner exchange agreement.

Free Samah Hijjawi and all Palestinian prisoners!

🇵🇸 Samidoun Euskal Herria
@samidoun_eus
Defender los "dos estados" es defender a la entidad sionista.

Occupation forces abducted Birzeit University student Rayan Maher Shtayyeh, from the village of Salem, east of Nablus, while passing through the Ein Siniya checkpoint, north of Ramallah, this afternoon.

Freedom for Rayan and all Palestinian students!

For the third consecutive time, the Zionist occupation "military courts" renewed the administrative detention — imprisonment without charge or trial — order against the Hebron University student in the Faculty of Media, Ahmed Al-Sharif, for another 6 months.

He is the brother of the martyr Malik Al Sharif.

Free Ahmed al-Sharif and all Palestinian students!

Myassar Al-Faqih, Palestinian feminist and leftist activist, was abducted by Zionist occupation forces from her home in Nablus on Thursday, 26 September 2024. Myassar, 60, is married to land defender and popular activist Wael Alfaqeeh and the mother of four daughters and one son. She is currently being held in Damon Prison. Myassar is a member of the Administrative Council of the General Union of Palestinian Women and a member of the Board of Directors of the Health Work Committees.

 In 2006, she ran for the Palestinian Legislative Council. She is also active in supporting Palestinian women economically through initiatives like "Your Voice is a Memory," which aims to showcase Palestinian heritage through handmade products, such as embroidery and traditional foods. She often speaks publicly in Palestine and internationally about the struggle of Palestinian women, especially the prisoners; photo 3 above is of her speaking in Berlin in 2020.

Myassar’s husband, Wael, has been arrested on at least four occasions by the occupation forces for his involvement in popular resistance and land defense activities confronting illegal settlement projects. He also works with Myassar to support local women’s cooperatives growing za’atar in the villages around Nablus, helping to support land cultivation by Palestinians and supporting women’s economic independence.

Speaking in Berlin, Myassar said: “Palestinian women are mothers, sisters, prisoners, martyrs, resistors, heroes, the steadfast and the wounded, the nurturer of the resistance fighters, the ones present on the front lines and the teachers of love of the homeland. Their social rights increase the strength of their national role.”

Freedom for Myassar Al-Faqih and all Palestinian prisoners in Zionist jails!

Shortly after her abduction, the colonial occupation authority issued an Administrative Detention order for Abla Sa'adat, Palestinian activist and the wife of Ahmad Sa'adat, Palestinian prisoner, national leader and the General Secretary of the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine. 

This means she is held without charge or trial under a so-called "secret file." The order is for 4 months but can be extended indefinitely. Palestinians are routinely jailed for years at a time under administrative detention, and there are currently over 3,600 — out of nearly 10,000 — Palestinian prisoners held under these orders, first introduced to Palestine by British colonialism and later adopted by the Zionist regime.

Free Abla Sa'adat and all Palestinian prisoners!

Hands off Students Supporting Palestine: Against the repression of the Popular University of Brussels!

On 7 May 2024, dozens of youth occupied a university building at ULB (Brussels, Belgium) and inaugurated the Popular University of Brussels. This encampment followed months of mobilization from students and activists, with the demand of an academic boycott of zionist institutions.

For nearly 2 months, the Walid Daqqa building, honoring the memory of the Palestinian freedom fighter, scholar, and martyr, had been a place for political discussions, conferences, events, demonstrations, conviviality, and activism. The student movement in Brussels organized at Walid Daqqa building and several other places, and developed a strong political position against normalization with the zionist entity.

During this time, the encampment has been criminalized both by the university authorities and the zionist movement in Belgium.  

During several demonstrations, police repression took place. At dawn on 25 June, the encampment was violently evicted by police, who stormed the building, attacking people inside while they slept and identifying everyone.

A few weeks later, nearly 100 people have been summoned for judicial hearings in relation to the encampment.

This judicial procedure is an attempt to intimidate people and discourage their just cause.  The summon letters mention that people will be interrogated on the basis of “being part of a group advocating racial segregation or discrimination.”

We refuse and condemn the criminalization of the student movement. Youth worldwide have been protesting against genocide and colonization, and have used the right of their freedom of expression to advocate for an academic boycott. The repression of their activism must not be tolerated.

From Brussels to Gaza:
Student Intifada!
 Solidarity until liberation!
HANDS OFF STUDENTS!

Free Mahmoud Issa! 

Mahmoud Issa was born in the town of Anata, northeast of Jerusalem, on May 21, 1968. He studied at the College of Sharia and Fundamentals of Religion at Al-Quds University, worked for the “Voice of  Truth and Freedom” newspaper and joined the Islamic Bloc at the University. He became involved in military action with the outbreak of the Stone Intifada,  joining the Al-Qassam Brigades, 
the military wing of Hamas, and establishing its 
first military cell in al-Quds.

His group, “Special Unit 101,” carried out several  military operations, including  capturing an occupation sergeant on 13 December 1992. They demanded that the occupation authorities free Hamas leader Sheikh Ahmed Yassin in exchange. 

The occupation refused their demands and classified the operation as “the most dangerous capture operation in the history of the Palestinian resistance.”

In the following days, the Zionist regime attempted to exile 415 Hamas and Islamic Jihad officials and members to Marj al-Zuhour in southern Lebanon. In 1993, at the age of 25, Issa was detained and tortured by the occupation in the infamous “Al-Maskobiyya” center but refused to confess to anything. After his “trial” he faced solitary confinement  in Ramle prison  and then was  transferred and isolated again in Bir al-Saba and Asqalan prisons.  He is sentenced to three life sentences plus 49 years in Zionist jails.

In 1996 Issa and a group of prisoners dug a 10 meter underground tunnel in order to escape, but the occupation authorities discovered the tunnel and extended his sentence by 6 years. Issa was then transferred on average every 6 months from one prison to another in an attempt to undermine his resolve. 

In 2002, during the Al-Aqsa Intifada, the imprisoned Issa was brought back to interrogation, and his elderly mother was brought in for interrogation in order to pressure him, after her house was raided by the Israeli intelligence and army. More years were added to his sentence on charges of communicating with the Hamas leadership abroad and participating in organizing  resistance youth.

Mahmoud Issa was then ordered to isolation by occupation prison authorities, where he was held for over 11 years consecutively. He is one of the few prisoners who spent such a major percentage of his detention in isolation, as the occupation ranks him among the most “dangerous” prisoners as an influential leader. His release is a demand of the Palestinian resistance in a prisoner exchange, and the Zionist regime has refused to release him in past exchanges, alongside fellow leaders Hassan Salameh, Ahmad Sa’adat, Marwan Barghouti,  Abbas al-Sayyed, and Ibrahim Hamed. 

After the Al-Karama hunger strike in 2012, in which thousands of prisoners joined a mass strike with a major demand being the release of imprisoned leaders from isolation, the occupation was forced to return him to the general prison population. The occupation prevented him from completing his university education at the Hebrew University, along with his fellow prisoners. Yet the years of imprisonment did not discourage the him from learning and writing over six books and short story compilations, including “Resistance Between  Theory and  Practice,” “Saber’s Story,” and “Quranic Reflections.”

Mahmoud Issa was denied the ability to complete his degree at Al-Quds University because he was wanted by the occupation and, since then, has been imprisoned in occupation jails. He is a prominent leader of the prisoners’ movement, and an example of the student prisoners, who include not only those serving in administrative detention but also those with lengthy sentences for their involvement and leadership in struggle. 

Mahmoud Issa (Abu al-Bara’a) remains a leader of the Palestinian prisoners’ movement after 31 years behind bars. Free Mahmoud Issa and all Palestinian prisoners!

The eternal martyr Basil Al-Araj wrote abundantly before his ascension in March of 2017. In his posthumously published book, "I Have Found My Answers," (a line from his will) he spoke of war during the 2014 zionist aggression on Gaza, just prior to the IOF ground invasion on July 17.

Basil guided us with eight rules and insights on the nature of war. He wrote:
—
Since there is talk of a ground operation, several points must be considered:

1. The Palestinian resistance consists of guerrilla formations whose strategies follow the logic of guerrilla warfare or hybrid warfare, which Arabs and Muslims have become masters of through our experiences in Afghanistan, Iraq, Lebanon, and Gaza. War is never based on the logic of conventional wars and the defense of fixed points and borders; on the contrary, you draw the enemy into an ambush. You do not stick to a fixed position to defend it; instead, you perform maneuvers, movement, withdrawal, and attack from the flanks and the rear. So, never measure it against conventional wars.

2. The enemy will spread photos and videos of their invasion into Gaza, occupation of residential buildings, or presence in public areas and well-known landmarks. This is part of the psychological warfare in guerrilla wars; you allow your enemy to move as they wish so that they fall into your trap and you strike them. You determine the location and timing of the battle. So, you may see photos from Al-Katiba Square, Al-Saraya, Al-Rimal, or Omar Al-Mukhtar Street, but do not let this weaken your resolve. The battle is judged by its overall results, and this is merely a show.

3. Never spread the occupation's propaganda, and do not contribute to instilling a sense of defeat. This must be focused on, for soon, we will start talking about a massive invasion in Beit Lahia and Al-Nusseirat, for example. Never spread panic; be supportive of the resistance and do not spread any news broadcast by the occupation (forget about the ethics and impartiality of journalism; just as the zionist journalist is a fighter, so are you).

4. The enemy may broadcast images of prisoners, most likely civilians, but the goal is to suggest the rapid collapse of the resistance. Do not believe them.

5. The enemy will carry out tactical, qualitative operations to assassinate some symbols [of resistance], and all of this is part of psychological warfare. Those who have died and those who will die will never affect the resistance's system and cohesion because the structure and formations of the resistance are not centralized but horizontal and widespread. Their goal is to influence the resistance's support base and the families of the resistance fighters, as they are the only ones who can affect the men of the resistance.

6. Our direct human and material losses will be much greater than the enemy's, which is natural in guerrilla wars that rely on willpower, the human element, and the extent of patience and endurance. We are far more capable of bearing the costs, so there is no need to compare or be alarmed by the magnitude of the numbers.

7. Today's wars are no longer just wars and clashes between armies but rather are struggles between societies. Let us be like a solid structure and play a game of biting fingers with the enemy, our society against their society.

Finally, every Palestinian (in the broad sense, meaning anyone who sees Palestine as a part of their struggle, regardless of their secondary identities), every Palestinian is on the front lines of the battle for Palestine, so be careful not to fail in your duty.
This is just as relevant for Lebanon as it is for Palestine.

Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah: The martyrdom of a great international revolutionary leader of our era (pt 4)

On the road to Al-Quds, on the road to victory and liberation

The pain of Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah’s assassination is felt in the heart of every free human who cherishes liberation, justice and a future of dignity for all. His revolutionary honesty and clarity, brilliant strategic wisdom,  and deep commitment is cherished everywhere, from the villages, cities and refugee camps of Palestine, where spontaneous marches burst into the streets at the news of his martyrdom, to the popular cradle of south Lebanon and among the Lebanese people as a whole, to those who march for Palestine and against the genocide in the centre of Johannesburg, South Africa, to a commune in Venezuela working to build popular solidarity and confront U.S. imperialism, to even the streets and campuses of the imperial core, where mass movements confront genocide, imperialism and Zionism. He represents not only himself, but the true promise of the resistance, for victory, return and liberation of Palestine from the river to the sea, for the defeat of Zionism, imperialism and their reactionary agents and partners. Committed always to uplifting the oppressed, Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah lived his life as a mujahid on the road to al-Quds, and a true international revolutionary leader of our time.

The Zionist and imperialist forces seek to declare an illusory “victory” or “achievement” over the Resistance through the assassination of Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah and the mass targeting of the Lebanese and Palestinian people. However, despite their genocidal campaigns of aerial bombing, they have been utterly unable to uproot or destroy the Resistance and its deep commitment among the people. Despite their genocidal attempts to erase the revolution and the resistance in a sea of blood, they will never kill the resistance nor the Palestinian, Arab, Islamic and international revolution against Zionism and imperialism. 

We hold the deepest confidence that the unified Resistance will ensure that the occupation is held accountable for its genocidal aggression and its cowardly assassinations. For those of us in the imperial core, this is a moment to escalate our struggle, to organize more actively, to take direct action, to fill the streets, and to make it impossible for imperialist business as usual to continue. 

As the martyr Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah said in his last speech, "The end of this battle will be a historic victory." Our collective movement must, with full confidence and commitment, do everything in our power to ensure the correctness and inevitability of this statement.  

This great crime will only inspire even more resistance and struggle along the path set out and exemplified by Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah, the great leader and the great martyr, until the defeat of the Zionist regime and its imperialist partners and sponsors, until victory:  the liberation of Palestine from the river to the sea, and the liberation of the Arab people and the region from Zionism, imperialism and their agents and collaborators.

Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah: The martyrdom of a great international revolutionary leader of our era (pt 3)

From Ibrahim Aqil to Fouad Shukr to Abbas al-Musawi, from Ismail Haniyeh to Saleh al-Arouri to  Fathi Shiqaqi, Abu Ali Mustafa, Abdel-Aziz Rantisi, Sheikh Ahmed Yassin, Imad Mughniyyeh, Yahya Ayyash, Abu Jihad, Kamal ‘Udwan, Mohammed al-Najjar, Basil al-Kubaisi, Kamal Nasser, Wadie Haddad, Ghassan Kanafani, Mohammed Boudia, Basil al-Araj, Tariq Izzedine to Samir Kuntar; the Zionist regime relies on the assassination weapon against the liberation movement. Sayyed Hassan Nasrallah: The martyrdom of a great international revolutionary leader of our era (part 1)

“I assure all of you: to the enemy, to the friend, to the whole world: You cannot eliminate Hezbollah, nor will you be able to eliminate the honourable resistance movements in Palestine. Palestinian student Malak Ziad Al-Amer was abducted by Zionist occupation forces at the illegal occupation checkpoint of “Doutan”, southwest of the occupied Palestinian city of Jenin, on Wednesday, 25 September 2024, only an hour after her family home was demolished in Jenin refugee camp. 

Malak is the sister of Nidal Ziad Al-Amer, who was assassinated by occupation forces on 3 July 2024. Occupation forces, disguised in a civilian car with a Palestinian license plate, invaded Jenin’s industrial area and assassinated Nidal, shooting him dead.

Nidal, 23, was a leader in Jenin refugee camp at the time of his assassination, following in the footsteps of his and Malak’s father, Ziad Al-Amer, who was one of the leaders of the Jenin battle of 2002. Nidal was pursued by the occupation, deeply involved in the resistance in Jenin, battling the occupying forces invading the camp and city and targeting their “Nimer” armored personnel carrier with explosives. 

Nidal was also a former prisoner, who was abducted by the occupation on at least three occasions, including in 2022 and 2018. The demolition of his and Malak’s family home today is part and parcel of the policy of collective punishment and targeting of the families of the martyrs, prisoners and resistance fighters pursued by the occupation. 

Ziad Al-Amer, Malak and Nidal’s father, was a renowned leader of the struggle in Jenin, one of the four commanders of the Palestinian resistance to the occupation forces in 2002 as a leader of Al-Aqsa Martyrs’ Brigades. Martyred in the battle of Jenin, Ziad Al-Amer was a close comrade of Zakaria Al-Zubaidi, today imprisoned as one of the heroes of the Freedom Tunnel Gilboa prison escape of 2021. 

The Al-Amer family are refugees from Al-Mansi, near Haifa, and were forced from their homes and lands by the Zionist occupation in the 1947-1948 Nakba. They have been denied their right to return to their homes since that time, and have unremittingly struggled for the liberation of Palestine’s land and people. 

Malak and Nidal’s cousins, Ayham and Ayser Al-Amer, were also both martyred in the struggle against the occupation. Both resistance fighters defending Jenin and struggling for the liberation of Palestine, Ayser was martyred on 26 October 2023 and Ayham only two weeks later, on 9 November 2023. Their brother, also named Ziad, is currently imprisoned by the occupation. 

Both Ayser, a founder of the Jenin Brigades, and Ayham, were liberated Palestinian prisoners. Ayser spent 5 years in occupation prisons and was last released only months before his martyrdom. Ayham spent 7 years behind Zionist bars and was also detained and tortured by the Palestinian Authority under “security coordination” with the Zionist regime before he escaped from PA detention in 2022. 

The abduction of Malak Ziad Al-Amer, on the same day as the destruction of her family home, is part and parcel of the policy of collective punishment of the Al-Amer family for their steadfast commitment to the liberation of Palestine and their tremendous sacrifices in order to achieve that goal. 

Freedom for Malak Al-Amer and all Palestinian prisoners in Zionist jails!

Zakyeh Shammout: A pioneer resistance fighter

Zakyeh Shammout, “Um Masoud”, was born in Haifa in 1945 and joined the guerrilla resistance action of the Fateh movement in 1968, at the age of 23, becoming one of the first Palestinian Fedayeen to carry out armed resistance operations against the Israeli occupation.

Between the end of the 1960s and the beginning of the 1970s, Zakyeh completed about 7 commando operations.

One of her most prominent operations consisted of placing an explosive device inside a large watermelon emptied of its contents. She wore a coat stuffed with dynamite and carried her nine-month-old son with one hand, and the device inside a basket with the other hand. She placed the basket strategically in a Bulgarian circus in Haifa, where the bomb exploded, killing an Israeli soldier.

Zakyeh was arrested in 1972 with her parents and husband while travelling from Syria to Palestine. At that time, she was 5 months pregnant.

During her pregnancy she was subjected to violent torture, from severe beatings to electric shocks. She gave birth to her child while in solidarity confinement on the floor of her cell. The door was only opened as blood was rushing out from under it. Zakyeh had fainted and her child was already born and crying. It was fellow Palestinian freedom fighter Fatima Bernawi, a trained nurse, who saved Zakyeh and her baby during the birth.

She named her baby girl Nadia after her companion in captivity, Moroccan activist Nadia El Baradly, who was with her in the same prison after carrying out a commando operation against Israelis.

Zakyeh and her husband were released in the great 1985 prisoner exchange, often called the “Jibril agreement,” in which 1150 prisoners were exchanged with three Israelis that were captured by the PFLP-GC in Lebanon. Israel first refused to include her in the deal but eventually accepted after escalated Palestinian pressure. Other prisoners freed in this exchange included Sheikh Ahmed Yassin, later the leading founder of Hamas; and Ziad Nakhaleh, now the General Secretary of Palestinian Islamic Jihad.

Zakyeh and her husband were then forcibly deported to Algeria without their children, who were instead kept in orphanages. Their children were initially prevented from seeing their parents and threatened to have their Israeli passports revoked if they wished to see their mother. Some later joined her in Algeria, others remained in Palestine. Zakyeh continued her struggle for liberation from the diaspora until she passed away in Algeria on September 16, 2014.

In video 8 above, you can see Zakyeh in Algeria, in 2009,  speaking about the Zionist attack on Gaza.

Her words are just as resonant in 2024.

10 years after her martyrdom, we remember her life and her legacy as a pioneer resistance fighter. From Zakyeh Shammout to Shadia Abu Ghazaleh, Etaf Alayan, Wafaa Idriss, Dareen Abu Eisheh, to the women of today’s resistance, the struggle continues until return and liberation.

🔴 Zainab Sajdiya sentenced to 12 months in prison by occupation forces

On the night of November 26-27, the colonial forces raided the house of Zainab Abu Sajdiya (50 years old), in Dheisheh camp, Bethlehem, arresting her without giving any reason. She was handcuffed, blindfolded and taken for interrogation. On arrival at the sinister Hasharon prison, she was beaten by the guards and strip-searched. Her lawyer was able to gather her testimony and her account of the treatment she endured from the very first days of her detention.

“During the search, I was beaten and there was a glass of water that was thrown at me. One of the female soldiers ordered me to go out while I was almost naked, but I refused. She tore my jacket. I wanted to close it, but she prevented me. 
Then they put me in a room with other female prisoners that lacked the most basic necessities of life. It was cold and had no mattress or blankets. The prison guard would come in and beat us. She slapped me and hit me with her fist all over my body. I received harsh blows to my stomach, so I felt that I could no longer breathe. 
They also tightened the handcuffs on my hands so tightly that they swelled”

Zainab Abu Sajdiya was then transferred to Damon, where she is locked up alongside 97 other Palestinian women prisoners. She was diagnosed with cancer in 2009, and her family fears that her condition will deteriorate as a result of the harsh conditions under which Palestinian prisoners are incarcerated, and the Zionist state's policy of deliberate medical negligence in its prisons. 

On July 16, after just under 10 months of detention without trial, Zionist military courts sentenced Zainab Abu Sajdiya to 12 months' imprisonment and a 7,000 shekel fine.
She is the mother of 5 children, 2 of whom are currently being held in Zionist prisons: Muhannad and Rani, who is injured.

🔴 Doaa Al-Qadi, Tahreer Jaber and Abla Sa'adat kidnapped by occupation in Ramallah raids!

On the night of September 16-17, the occupying forces raided various West Bank towns, arresting over 30 people, beating them up and destroying their homes. Among the 30 arrested were 3 women, including 2 well-known activists and 1 student from Birzeit university.

In Al Bireh, the colonial army arrested Abla Saadat, wife of Popular Front Secretary General Ahmad Saadat, at her home with her daughter, when some twenty soldiers burst into the family home. In 2003, Abla had already been arrested on her way to Brazil to take part in the World Social Forum as an Addameer representative. She was transferred to administrative detention for 4 months and launched a hunger strike with two other administrative detainees to protest against their isolation and demand their release.

Ahmad Saadat is Secretary General of the Popular Front, one of the historic components of the Palestinian Resistance and the main left-wing force in Palestine. In 2002, he was arrested by the Palestinian Authority on the orders of the Zionist state and imprisoned in Jericho. In 2006, the Zionist army raided the prison where he was being held and kidnapped him along with 4 of his comrades, Ahed Abu Ghoulmeh, Majdi al-Rimawi, Basel al-Asmar and Hamdi Quran. In 2008, a Zionist military court sentenced him to 30 years in prison.

In the same raid, zionist occupation forces abducted Tahreer Badran Jaber from her home in Beitunia. Tahreer Jaber is a longtime Palestinian feminist activist & organizer, best-known for her work with the Union of Palestinian Women's Committees of which she is Executive Director. She is also a member of the World March of Women.

The Union of Palestinian Women's Committees (UPWC) is a feminist, popular, civil and progressive organization founded in 1980. It is one of 6 NGOs classified as banned organizations by the Zionist state in 2021, along with Addameer, Al Haq, Bisan, UAWC, and Defense for Children Palestine. In November 2020, Khitam Saafin, UPWC's previous director, was arrested and imprisoned for 18 months before being released in May 2022.

Duaa Al-Qadi is a student in the Faculty of Media of of Bir Zeit university. She was abducted from her home in the city of Al-Bireh. Her brother Mustafa, also a student in Bir Zeit university, is in detention in occupation jail since May 2023. Their other brother Mohammad was also arrested once, then released, and now waits for his two siblings.

🚨 A number of prisoners recently released from "Ramon Prison" informed the family of the prisoner and engineer Montaser Mustafa Al-Shannar, from Nablus, who has been under administrative detention for 25 months, that he is suffering from a severe skin disease, scabies, which he developed in May 2024 while incarcerated in Naqab Prison.

Palestinian Lawyer Diala Ayesh ordered to 4 more months of administrative detention

Diala Nader Ibrahim Eideh (Diala Ayesh), a lawyer from Al-Bireh and a dedicated advocate for Palestinian political prisoners, was ordered for the third time on 12 September 2024 to an additional four months in administrative detention, arbitrary imprisonment without charge or trial. She was abducted by colonial Zionist occupation forces  on 17 January 2024 at the “Container” checkpoint north of Bethlehem. 

 On 24 January, the Zionist occupation military commander in the West Bank issued an administrative detention order against her for four months. On 23 May 2024, the Ofer military court renewed her administrative detention for a further four months -- now once again renewed in order to keep her behind bars. 

Diala is a lawyer and human rights defender. She actively monitors and documents the living conditions of Palestinian political prisoners in the colonial military prison system and in the Palestinian Authority prisons. After 7 October 2023, when Israel launched its ongoing assault on the besieged Gaza Strip, Diala formed a volunteer collective of women lawyers to support Palestinians subjected to ongoing mass arrests.

Even after her arrest, after she was ordered jailed without charge or trial, she continued to advocate for her fellow Palestinian political prisoners, her sister Aseel recalled in an interview with Al-Jazeera. She noted that Diala sent a message with her lawyer urging her to contact the family members of several male prisoners with updates.

Diala asked Aseel to give these families the latest updates on their sons, which she had written onto a notepad.

“She’s in prison, and we don’t know anything about her – whether she’s eating or not, sleeping or not, or what conditions she is being held in,” said Aseel. “Yet, all of her worries are to pass on a message from a male prisoner to his fiancee outside.”

Lawyers’ and human rights organizations around the world have urged Diala’s release, including Front Line Defenders, the International Association of Democratic Lawyers, Protect-Lawyers, Lawyers for Justice, Lawyers for Lawyers, and IBAHRI. The UN Special Rapporteur on the situation of Human Rights Defenders, Mary Lawlor, also urged her immediate release.

Diala Ayesh is one of 95 Palestinian women prisoners, including 24 held without charge or trial under “administrative detention.” There are approximately 9900 Palestinian prisoners, not counting potentially thousands of Palestinians abducted from Gaza and held in occupation torture camps. Of the Palestinian prisoners, over 3600 are jailed without charge or trial under these indefinitely renewable orders. Administrative detention was first introduced into Palestine by the British colonial mandate and has been adopted by the zionist regime as a mechanism to attack and repress Palestinian community leaders.

When Diala was first detained, she was held in solitary confinement and denied access to a lawyer. Palestinian prisoners, including women prisoners, are routinely denied family visits, deprived of access to lawyers, deprived of sufficient food, denied access to recreation and are held in dangerous and unsanitary conditions posing a risk to health and life. Diala, who consistently advocates for Palestinian political prisoners in Zionist jails -- and prisoners of the Palestinian Authority under “security coordination” with the Israeli regime -- is now continuing her fight for justice from behind prison bars alongside her fellow detainees.

Free Diala Ayesh and all Palestinian prisoners!

This summer, the International Women's Alliance presented its Women of Valor award at its Malaysia assembly to women whose principles & commitment move forward liberation struggles, including Etaf Alayan, a heroine of the Palestinian liberation movement over decades of struggle.  Etaf sent a video, to receive the award, as she has been forbidden by the occupation from traveling outside Palestine for years now, and the award was accepted on her behalf by Palestinian women and supporters of Palestine:

"In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful,

“Permission [to fight] has been given to those who are being fought because they were wronged, and indeed, Allah is able to grant them victory.”

Allah the Almighty has spoken the truth.
To the esteemed sisters and brothers present, to the dear and hospitable country of Malaysia, my greetings to you.

Greetings to you, and Um Yusuf is still searching for her curly-haired son.

Greetings to you, and the girl is still searching for her mother, whom she recognizes by her hair among the martyrs.

Greetings to you, and the fighters are still defending their land.

Greetings to you, and a mother in Jenin buries her four martyred sons and is shy in her mourning in front of the catastrophe in Gaza.

Greetings to you, and the nails of the people of the West Bank have been clipped under Oslo, in protection of the settler herds that roam and corrupt our land.
 
Greetings to you, and the free women of Palestine  are still being tortured in prisons but remain unsilenced.

Greetings to you, and the doors of Al-Aqsa are closed to its lovers and devotees,  but open to the herds of settlers who desecrate it

Greetings to you, and the rulers of the so-called super powers bury their heads like ostriches, deaf to the truth and seeing only what the occupying entity shows them,  repeating its claim of "Israel's right to defend itself."

Greetings to you, and the Arab rulers were castrated, with their voice nowhere to be heard.

Greetings to you, and the Islamic countries still do not see their duty towards the land that Allah has blessed and claimed to himself those who defend it, as He said: "We sent against you servants of Ours of great might."

Greetings to you, and to the living consciences that refused to be like a herd behind their rulers, those shouting no to injustice, and demanding freedom for Palestine. These consciences will be the safety valve to their countries, 
for those who cry out against injustice.. are alive.

Greetings to the unity of the fields, the happy Yemen with its decision, the proud Iraq, Iran the supporter, and the great Hezbollah, victorious by the will of Allah.

Greetings to the resistance fighters from all backgrounds in Gaza the proud, Jenin the steadfast, Tulkarem the dignified, Qabatiya the heroic, Nablus the mountain of fire, and to everyone who broke their silence to defend their land and protect their honor.

Despite all the pain and wounds that have afflicted the entire Palestinian body, our cause is not just a humanitarian issue; it is a matter of an occupied homeland, a lost right.

"And Allah is predominant over His affair, but most of the people do not know."

We trust in Allah's victory.

Greetings to the wounded Sudan, the injured Syria, and to the Philippines to Vietnam, greetings to all the oppressed peoples from the north to the south of the earth, and from east to west.

Greetings to you, and peace be upon the people of Gaza for their patience.  They have indeed been afflicted as Allah said: "And We will surely test you with something of fear and hunger and a loss of wealth and lives
and livelihood, but give good tidings to the patient."

So rejoice, O patient ones, in Allah's victory.

Greetings to you, and peace be upon you."

Walaa Tanja from Balata camp (Nablus) receives new administrative detention order of 6 months

Walaa Khaled Tanja grew up in Balata refugee camp (Nablus), in a family with a strong history of resistance for Palestinian liberation. At the age of 15, she received her mother, Latifa Abu Dara, on Al-Quds Street near the Huwarra checkpoint, when she was released as part of the Wafaa Al-Ahrar prisoner exchange in 2011.

Her mother was later martyred due to cancer and her brother has also been repeatedly pursued, imprisoned and wounded by occupation soldiers.

Walaa was arrested on August 20th 2022, with Tahrir Abu Sariya and Maryam Arafat, and accused of attempting to carry out an armed operation in retaliation for the murder of Ibrahim Al Nabulsi. The occupation claimed that they opened fire on soldiers at the "Kedumim" illegal colonial settlement near the occupied Palestinian cities of Nablus and Qalqilya.

Walaa was released on November 24th 2023, as part of the first batch of the Al-Aqsa Flood prisoner exchange deal, in which 13 Israeli women and children were exchanged for 39 Palestinian women and children who were held hostage by the occupation. 

This interrupted Walaa’s original 7-year sentence and she was welcomed home by her family. However, due to the heavy repression by the occupation, no big celebration was possible.

After her release, Walaa expressed her feelings about her liberation thanks to the Resistance in Gaza. (video above)

On March 12th 2024, while she was arriving to Tulkarem from Nablus together with her 16-year-old cousin, occupation special forces attacked the car with their guns pointed at them, and seized Walaa once again. She was taken to a military camp, then to Hasharon crossing and then to Damon prison.

Her abduction clearly broke the prisoner exchange agreement, which has now been done several times by the occupation.

Walaa was ordered to six months in “administrative detention” -- arbitrary imprisonment without charge or trial, indefinitely renewable. On 11 September 2024, her detention was extended for another six months. We urge immediate liberation for Walaa Khaled Tanja, together with the liberation of all Palestinian prisoners.

She was shot by a Zionist sniper in Beita on Friday, September 6th as she confronted the land-thieving settler mobs as part of the weekly demonstrations to defend the land of Palestine.

Aysenur committed her life to the Palestinian cause and for the liberation of Palestine from US imperialism. She was martyred using weaponry provided to the Zionists by the American imperialists. The US government has the blood of Aysenur on its hands, as well as the tens of thousands of martyrs in Gaza and the West Bank.

Aysenur was involved in fighting against the same institutions that bought the bullets that killed her. She participated in the student intifada, organizing at the encampment at the University of Washington, demanding the University divest from "Israel" and to cut ties with Boeing. Boeing, the 3rd largest weapons corporation in the world, is responsible for sending billions of dollars of munitions to the occupation forces. These include Joint Direct Attack Munitions (JDAMs), which were used in multiple horrific  massacres in Gaza, killing hundreds of Palestinians. Another US weapons company, General Dynamics, produces the MK-8 bombs, used by the Zionist forces in the horrific massacre in the al-Mawasi camp in which the IOF bombed displaced Palestinians residing in tents, creating 27-foot-deep craters in the sand. As we honour Aysenur, we must also commit ourselves to building and developing the student Intifada and confronting the war machine at all US and Western universities.

Aysenur is one of the many martyrs who have given their lives for the Palestinian liberation struggle, and she is one of the many internationals who have made sacrifices in solidarity with Palestine, including fellow internationalist martyrs for Palestine Rachel Corrie, Tom Hurndall, and the martyrs of the 2010 Gaza Freedom Flotilla: Furkan Doğan, Cengiz Akyüz, Ali Haydar Bengi, İbrahim Bilgen, Cevdet Kılıçlar, Cengiz Songür, Çetin Topçuoğlu, Fahri Yaldız, Necdet Yıldırım and, Ugur Süleyman Söylemez.

Fusako Shigenobu was jailed in Japan for over 21 years as a political prisoner for her, and her comrades' involvement in the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine's armed struggle. Inside Palestine, the prisoners of Palestine held by the Zionist regime over the years have included Terre Fleener, Brigit Schultz, Ludvina Jansen, Thomas Reuter, and Kozo Okamoto, who have spent years inside "Israeli" jails and participated as part of the Palestinian prisoners' movement.

This is not to mention the numerous Arab liberation strugglers who have given their lives and freedom for the liberation of Palestine, as we see daily in the Arab support fronts for Gaza stretching from Yemen to Lebanon to Iraq, Syria and beyond. Arab strugglers for Palestine, from Georges Abdallah, a Lebanese internationalist struggler, who fought against French imperialism and has been imprisoned in French prisons for over 40 years, to Mohammed Boudia, Mohamed Zouari and Mohammed Salah, are among countless Arab martyrs for Palestine who have viewed Palestine as the central cause of Arab liberation. Most recently, Arab popular commitment and sacrifice for Palestine, despite official normalization and complicity, has been represented by the martyr Maher al-Jazi, the Jordanian truck driver who carried out the Karameh operation on the Jordanian border with occupied Palestine, widely honoured as a "hero of Al-Aqsa Flood."

Through different eras of the Palestinian revolution and various forms of struggle, the ongoing legacy of internationalist commitment to Palestinian liberation comes in confrontation with the full imperialist partnership in Zionist genocide. 

Glory to Aysenur! Victory to Palestine, glory to the martyrs, defeat to Zionism and imperialism, liberation for Palestine from the river to the sea!

Brothers in Arms: Martyrs of Resistance and Oslo's Betrayal (3/3):
In 2023, mobility is not so easy. Thus, the strategies of the resistance had to change to reflect this. A new strategy has been adopted, in Jenin, in Nablus, in Tulkarem, Al-Khalil, and Areeha, quickly spreading to all parts of Palestine: there is no free entry, and if you enter, we will burn you. While some resistance fighters venture into the interior to send their message, this cohesive strategy has proven to be effective in the last two years.

Today, we not only approach the 30th anniversary of Oslo, but also the one year anniversary of the kidnapping by the PA of Musab Shtayyeh, who, like Adel and Imad, was also the most wanted man in the West Bank. Shtayyeh, the link between the West Bank and Gaza, is a member of the Lions' Den and Al-Qassam Brigades. He has been subjected to torture, under the guise of kidnapping him "for protection," a bold-faced and recurring PA lie. 

Despite the setback of his continued abduction orchestrated by the US and zionists, resistance persists. Today's youth face both the PA and the occupation, avenging martyr after martyr in their operations. They understand that we face two enemies: the zionist entity and the Authority that "participates in our annihilation," in the words of Basil. 

This story provides one lesson: The PA is a collaborating arm of the occupation, and it too must be refused. Basil taught us, "Every Palestinian must understand that any confrontation with the Authority is not a confrontation with a Palestinian, but a confrontation with a tool of the occupation," a quote that rings clear considering the context of Musab's arrest: PA repression led to clashes, and he raised his gun to defend himself without knowing if the aggressor was the IOF or PA. This simple ambiguity paradoxically creates clarity to understand the reality of the Palestinian situation.

Down with Oslo and the PA! Down with the zionist entity!
Resistance persists, united and advancing, until the dismantling of all oppressors.

Brothers in Arms (2/3):
Three years, and all the forces of the PA and occupation could not catch them. In a video, Adel stated that he does not want revenge against those who kill him, but only revenge against the zionist entity. This sentiment is repeated by the resistance today, who understands that bloodshed with brothers serves the enemy, even in light of the atrocities committed by the PA. "Our guns do not see except the minarets of Al-Quds;" Dr. Ramadan Shallah's eminent quote is repeated in various forms by the factions today.

On this day in 1998, the IOF claimed to have assassinated both Adel and Imad, right after the PA tracked Imad after his prison escape. Their fate was unknown, whether they were arrested or martyred. Despite torture being used on them both, they refused to confess and refused to provide information about their fellow resistance fighters.

In 2014, the IOF released their bodies, received by a crowd of thousands who never forgot the legacy of the heroic brothers. Adel was wearing the same shirt, 16 years after his martyrdom. 

When they were martyred, the Oslo Accords were just five years old. Today, as we approach the 30th anniversary of the ill-fated Accords, the PA's position on liberation has not changed; rather, it has more deeply entrenched itself in normalization and betrayal. 

In the year of Adel and Imad's martyrdom, the so-called separation wall did not exist. The geography of the land was markedly different; Palestinians were connected to their land, and colonial checkpoints were a ghost of what they are now. Watchtowers were smaller. Pegasus and Blue Wolf were non-existent. The surveillance state was not as present.

Brothers in Arms: Martyrs of Resistance and Oslo's Betrayal (1/3):
Today's story of resistance concerns two brothers, a changing landscape, and lessons from the past. 

The brothers, commanders Adel and Imad Awadallah, were each other's right arm. They led the Al-Qassam Brigades, inflicting fear upon the enemy for years, especially in 1996 when 49 zionists were killed in one week in the "Sacred Revenge" series of operations avenging Yahya Ayyash.

On this day in 1998, they were martyred on a farm, besieged by special forces in Al-Khalil. After hours of armed clashes, they ascended gloriously.

No one knew their fate for 16 years.
This is their story.

From Al-Bireh, Adel and Imad joined the Al-Qassam Brigades in their youth, quickly advancing in the ranks and becoming leaders in Ramallah. When the mastermind engineer Yahya Ayyash was martyred at the hands of zionists in January 1996, they sought to avenge his death in a series of painful anti-colonial operations. 

Adel and Imad were the #1 most wanted in the West Bank, not only by the occupation but also by its lapdog, the Palestinian Authority. For three years, Adel and Imad hid from them both. All who visited their home were arrested, and the PA launched a massive arrest campaign of Hamas cadre.

In August 1998, Imad was kidnapped by the PA and tortured in Areeha prison. In a heroic escape, he liberated himself from prison despite solitary confinement, but was tracked by the PA, who planted an electronic device on him.

Adel, on the other hand, was still a fugitive. Clinging to his aspirations of his people's imminent liberation, he continued to orchestrate resistance operations. During one of them, forces of the Palestinian Authority opened fire on a Renault 9 car, believing him to be inside with his family, unconcerned with the casualties. The PA Forces ended up killing an unrelated Palestinian woman instead, in a move reminiscent of the PA's thuggish behavior today, like their assassination of martyrs Nizar Banat, Mohammed Al-Banna, and Abdel Qader Zaqdah.

Samidoun is circulating the following press statement by Bassem Naim, member of the Hamas Political Bureau, in response to what the Washington Post published yesterday about US officials regarding the ceasefire negotiations in the Gaza Strip and a prisoner exchange: 

Yesterday, the Washington Post published a report regarding the ongoing ceasefire negotiations in Gaza. 

According to the newspaper, US officials stated that during the last week Hamas has presented in new conditions to mediators concerning the release criteria for Palestinian prisoners in exchange for Israeli captives. 

These new conditions, the official claim have poisoned the negotiations -- which had already stalled due to Netanyahu's new demands. In light of this we would like to clarify the following:

First, we appreciate the US official's acknowledgement that the negotiations have stalled due to Netanyahu's new demands. 

Second, Hamas has not presented any new conditions to the mediators neither on the issue of prisoners nor on any other matter. The movement reaffirms its commitment to what was agreed upon on July 2nd of this year, which was based on President Biden's proposal and the UN Security Council Resolution number 2735. We are ready to immediately negotiate the implementation measures for this deal.

Third, regarding the criteria for release of prisoners, we would like to emphasize that the movement has shown a great deal of flexibility to reach an agreement that protects our people. For example while Hamas initially demanded the release of 500 Palestinian prisoners in exchange for each Israeli female soldier, the movement later accepted the release of only 50 prisoners
for each soldier. 

However, it is important to point out that Netanyahu, in his new conditions regarding prisoners' release has backtracked even on the standards he presented in his proposal at the end of May this year. He now seeks to impose new conditions that would strip the agreement of the prisoner deal of its substance, turning it into a futile prisoner deal. 

Fourth, instead of evading responsibility by fabricating lies about new conditions from Hamas the US administration should pressure Netanyahu to retract his new demands and move forward with the immediate implementation of the agreement reached on July 2nd.

Fifth, an agreement or proposal that does not include a complete withdrawal from the Philadelphia Corridor, reopening of Rafah Crossing, the safe return of displaced civilians to their homes, a serious prisoner exchange deal, relief for our people, and reconstruction of the destroyed Gaza Strip will not be acceptable. 

The US administration will bear full responsibility for the failure of these negotiations to achieve their objectives if it fails to exert pressure on the Zionist government to stop its aggression against our people.

Thank you very much.

Free the ReSisters of Birzeit University! 

Mona Abu Hussein is a student and a mother, from Aabod village near Ramallah. She was abducted by the occupation forces on 5 March 2024. Her administrative detention -- arbitrary imprisonment without charge or trial -- has been renewed twice, on 4 June and on 4 September, each time for 3 months.

Shahad Owaida is a left-wing student activist. Originally from Beita, Nablus, she is a computer science student. She was arrested on March 27 after a rally at which she gave a moving and powerful speech. Fadia Barghouti, one of her fellow prisoners released on May 21, describes her as "as solid and tall as the mountains of Nablus". 

Layan Kayed is a sociology student and left-wing student activist in the Democratic and Progressive Student Pole. She was arrested on April 7 at her home and incarcerated for 4 months of administrative detention, which was extended on August 5 for a further 4 months. In all, Layan has already spent almost a year and a half in colonial prisons during her last two incarcerations. Layan has written numerous texts, which can be found on the Metras, Al Akhbar and Palestine Studies websites.

Amal Shujaia is a journalism student and secretary of the student council's cultural committee. Elected as a member of the university's main organization, the Wafa'a Islamic Bloc, which won the last student elections, she hosted several cultural and associative events throughout the year. She was arrested on June 6 and her trial has been postponed until September 9.

Wafa Nimr was secretary of the student council's technical committee for the year 2023. Along with Amal Shujaia and Salam Al Malki, she was one of the 3 women students elected to the Birzeit student council under the banner of the Wafa'a Islamic Bloc.

She was arrested on June 30 during a search of her house on the outskirts of Ramallah.

Shatha Jaraba was arrested on the night of August 15-16 with her brother, Abdul Rahman Jaraba, during a raid on their home in the village of Beitin, east of Al-Bireh.
A student at Birzeit University in Biochemistry and Biology, she was elected to the student council for the Wafaa' Islamic Bloc and held the position of Secretary of the Cultural Committee in the 2022 - 2023 academic year.

Dania Hanatsheh was arrested on August 19 by the occupying forces in Ramallah al-Tahta, occupied Palestine. Dania is a prisoner who was released last November, as part of the exchange agreement won by the Resistance and the people of Gaza. On August 26, Dania was transferred to administrative detention - imprisonment without charge or trial, renewable indefinitely - for a period of 4 months.

At least 3 former students of the university are also incarcerated in the colonial prison of Damon: Layan Nasir, arrested on April 7 and whose administrative detention has been extended for a further 4 months, Saja Muadi, arrested on April 18 and under administrative detention for a further 6 months, and finally Zeina Barbar, who was arrested on July 9 and whose father is a prisoner who spent over 20 years in colonial prisons. 

Alongside these students, two academics and employees of the faculty are also detained. Hadeel Shatara, a Palestinian educator and administrator of the graduate program at Birzeit University, and Khalida Jarrar, a feminist activist and former member of the Palestinian Legislative Council.  Hadeel Shatara was arrested on June 30 and transferred to administrative detention for 6 months. Khalida Jarrar was arrested on December 26 and transferred to 6 months' administrative detention, renewed on June 24 for a further 6 months.

War profiteers lose up to $500,000 after Vancouver activists block Maersk distribution facility
